lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Do you have a problem traveller?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I just wondered why you're wearing that outfit... Is it fancy dress?

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: No! It's for protection.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Protection from what?

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: The plague of course…

sections.title: Standard dialogue at the Ardougne Wall

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: I'm guarding the border to West Ardougne. No one except we mourners can pass through.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: The plague of course. We can't risk cross contamination.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What brought the plague to Ardougne?

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: It's all down to King Tyras of West Ardougne. It started came back from one of his visits to the lands west of here.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Some of his men must have unknowingly caught it out there and brought it back with them

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does he know how bad the situation is now?

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: If he did he wouldn't care. I believe he wants his people to suffer, he's an evil man.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Isn't that treason?

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: He's not my king.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are the symptoms of the plague?

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: The first signs are typical flu symptoms. These tend to be followed by severe nightmares, horrifying hallucinations which drive many to madness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds nasty.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: It gets worse. Next the victim's blood changes into a thick black tar-like liquid, at this point they're past help.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Their skin is cold to the touch, the victim is now brain dead. Their body however lives on driven by the virus, roaming like a zombie, spreading itself further wherever possible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've heard enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then, see you around.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Maybe...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: What are you up to?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just sight-seeing.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: This is no place for sight-seeing. Don't you know there's been a plague outbreak?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I had heard.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Then I suggest you leave as soon as you can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the advice.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Hmmm, how did you get over here? You're not one of this rabble. Ah well, you'll have to stay. Can't risk you going back now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's a mourner?

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: We're working for King Lathas of East Ardougne. He has tasked us with containing the accursed plague sweeping West Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: We also do our best to ease these people's suffering. We're nicknamed mourners because we spend a lot of time at plague victim funerals, no one else is allowed to risk attending.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: It's a demanding job, and we get little thanks from the people here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't got the plague though...

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Can't risk you being a carrier. That protective clothing you have isn't regulation issue. It won't meet safety standards.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a woman named Elena.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Ah yes, I've heard of her. A healer I believe. She must be mad coming over here voluntarily.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: I hear rumours she has probably caught the plague now. Very tragic, a stupid waste of life.
